directives a host page needs in order for the

builder.

readme.text:
Headless asset upload plugin for Anvilkit Studio.

## What it ships

-

for Studio registration and runtime upload handling.
-

for invoking the configured upload adapter and registering the validated result.
-
for reading the plugin runtime registry after
for storing validated asset metadata, with

React components for a host-rendered upload button, asset browser (with search + filter chips), command palette, and metadata editor.
- Reference upload adapters for tests and demos.

## Library management (search, tags, pagination)
returns a paginated, filtered slice of the registry:
Tags are auto-derived on every upload (kind + up to two filename tokens, capped at 3) and can be edited via
is the sidebar-facing pagination contract — remote sources can implement it to push search and pagination to the server. Sidebar consumers fall back to
is a streaming channel that fans

envelopes out to every subscriber alongside the inline upload listener.

## Trust model
is treated as untrusted input. Every adapter response is validated through

before it reaches the registry or IR-shaped data.

The default scheme set is **always**
is **off by default** in v1.0 — enable it explicitly per plugin instance:

Two further hardening checks run at validation time:

- **Path traversal.**

URLs.
- **IDN homoglyph.** Hostnames mixing Latin with a visually confusable script (Cyrillic or Greek) are rejected. Single-script IDN hosts such as
are always allowed. Hosts that legitimately need this combination can pass

| _not supported — write a custom resolver wrapper_ |

If your host needs an exotic scheme (

directly. The rationale for dropping arbitrary scheme strings: every scheme allowed at the boundary needs its own CSP / sanitization story — typed flags force that decision to be explicit.

## Content Security Policy
computes the minimum CSP directives the configured adapters need. Call it once at boot and merge the result into your existing CSP builder:
returned by the presign service points at a different origin than the presign endpoint itself, pass

so the advisor can split the directives correctly.

## Entry points

-

— headless runtime, validation, adapters, errors, CSP advisor.
-

— fixtures for downstream plugin tests.

## Batch upload behaviour
(default 3) uploads in parallel. Per-file failures surface via the listener as
envelopes; the returned promise resolves with the successful subset and **does not throw**. Hosts that need fail-fast semantics can pass

s thrown from the adapter abort the whole batch — pending files are not scheduled.

## S3 presigned adapter
back, then PUTs the file to

. Both phases retry on 5xx and network errors with exponential backoff. 4xx responses fail without retry.

If the host endpoint is **not** S3-compatible (e.g. a custom uploader without overwrite semantics), pass

to disable retry and avoid the small chance of a duplicate upload.

The adapter never logs file contents — only

.

## Production checklist

Before mounting in a real Studio host:

1. **Pick the right adapter.**
is the production default; wire it to your presign endpoint and set

.
2. **Set the trust flags explicitly.** Decide whether your host needs

off unless you have a documented business need.
3. **Wire CSP.** Call
and merge the result into your

builder. Re-run when you add or remove an adapter.
4. **Choose a persistence story.** The plugin keeps state in-memory per Studio mount. If you need cross-session asset reuse, the host stores
s server-side and re-seeds the registry on boot via

get separate alerts.
6. **Lock down the bundle.** Consult

— the headless entry stays under 6 KB gzip; the UI subpath stays under 12 KB. CI gates both.

## Error codes

| The registry has no entry for the asset id.       |
|

| The stored URL failed the allowlist or trust gate. |
|
